Look at this big smile on my face! This is the face I make when nothing in the whole world is bothering me!
If you want my advice, you should concentrate on your swings, or your little sister's gonna win easy-peasy!
My, those are big words, coming from you!
But big words don't win matches, skill does – and you're going to witness mine.
That's more like it. What do you say we have a quick warmup, then get right to it?
Sounds good. I'll be right back.
Lexington walked away. For a passing, lonesome moment, the golf course was silent.
BEEP... BEEP... BEEP...
There was an ever-so-faint beeping noise. A sound like a cruel clock, ticking down the seconds until death.
No, please, I'm begging you...
Make it stop...
Holiday songs echoed through the streets while neon lights and ribbons painted facades, street lights, and windows in the colors of Christmas.
Wow, look at all these stores offering Christmas deals and events! My wallet's gonna hate me for this, but who cares!
There really is a lot. Haven't you already bought enough for today, though?
No way! We've still got three blocks of stores to look at!
Besides, that Sakuran delegation is coming tomorrow, remember? We need to have gifts ready!
I've heard a lot about Amagi before. Honestly, I've always wanted to meet her!
Right. Amagi. With the Sakuran delegation...
If you'll indulge me... Do you really think she's coming?
Yes? We've been planning this for quite a while. It'd be omega rude to cancel at the last minute.
Why are you asking that out of nowhere?
Oh, no reason. I'm just so happy that it feels like I'm dreaming. Like this is all too good to be real.
Do you feel anything like that? At all?
L-Lex, come on... Don't ruin the mood...
There's no such thing as "real" or "fake" happiness.
As long as we're happy and the Commander is here with us, what more could you want?
Sara... I know something has been weighing heavily on you these past few days.
I'm your sister. I can tell when you're not telling me something.
That's enough spoiling the mood, okay? It's almost Christmas, so let's enjoy ourselves instead!
Hey, look, that's the Colorado sisters! Let's see what they bought!
Maryland! Colorado! West Virginia! Hiii!
Well, if it ain't Lex and Sara! Out doing your Christmas shopping too, eh?
Yup-yup! We're shopping our hearts out.
Ahahahah! Good to hear it. We've still got places to check out, so we'll catch up with you later! Merry Christmas!
Merry Christmas! Have fun!
See, Lex? Christmas is all about fun.
Maryland, Colorado, and West Virginia...
If the report I read was right, they were reassigned to DC's defense fleet to reward their stellar performance while serving at PH.
Is that really true?
What? Of course it's–
Sara. Look me in the eyes.
D-do I have to?
Yes. Listen to your heart and answer me.
Is that REALLY what happened?
No. It's not.
They... They were reassigned to PH not long before that Reenactment happened, and they were caught up in it.
They fought tooth and nail, but still sustained crippling injuries.
They spent a long time in recovery, but their wounds were too severe. In the end, they were mothballed.

Then there came a breakthrough in retrofitting technology that let their riggings be rebuilt. It wasn't until recently that they finally returned to the front lines and were assigned to posts across the country...
What about Yorktown? We threw a party with her not long ago.
Yorktown's hull became corroded after the Reenactment at AF. Her prospects of recovery have always been bleak.
That is, until the Commander used Type II technology to miraculously bring her back to full health.
She's finished her rehabilitation program at the Sea of Stars and should be back in fighting order.
Okay... And what about me?
Just... Just stop it with these questions, please.
BEEP... BEEP... BEEP...
The beeping was loud and clear.
It sounded even colder and crueler than usual.
What has become of me?
Am I leading a happy life together with you, far away from the front lines?
Shouldn't we all? What's wrong with being happy?!
Calm down, Sara. Answer me.
Lexington... You...
You suffered severe corrosion damage in the Reenactment at the Coral Sea.
You haven't made any recovery from your injuries, and there's still no known way to cure you...
To this day you're still resting in a care pod in the Sea of Stars...
It's not fair...
*sniffle*... They all recovered one way or another... but you're still sick...
Why? Why won't you come back to me?
I don't know why.
All I know is that you can't stay here forever.
All dreams must come to an end.
But...
Open your eyes, Sara.
Your friends need you. They need you with them in reality. The Commander is waiting for you, too.
I'm sure you already know that.
Yes, I know...
I know it perfectly well...
I know what I have to do. I have to keep fighting.
As long as I keep fighting, one day, things will change...
BEEP... BEEP... BEEP...
There was a barely audible beeping noise. Steady and cold, like the waves of a dead sea lapping against a barren beach.
I'm going to leave now, Lex.
You're right about everything. There are people in the real world that need me.
TB, do you copy?
This is TB. Awaiting orders.
Great! I'm gonna head to the control room. Find out what's happening in the Sea of Stars meanwhile.
We're going to start fighting back.

News From the East.say: The Dragon Empery's plan is to destroy the Storm. As such, they've codenamed their operation, "Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon."
This operation has been in the works for a long time. The chain of command across all of the Empery are now performing the final preparations.
That includes me, the one who'll lead the fleet.
There's one thing I've been hung up on, however.
(As far as the briefing says, even though the Storm has grown in intensity, the Empery has the strength to snuff it out.)
(If they succeed, they'll finally regain direct access to the sea.)
(But, my bad feeling from earlier proved right. The Pacific has fallen into chaos.)
(The Empery might get caught in the crossfire if they proceed with the operation, not knowing any better.)
Since the storm has picked up, it's basically impossible to get info on what's happening beyond the Empery's borders.
At least thanks to you, we've made contact with the Northern Parliament's base at Karaginsky Island.
Speaking of which, I have Sovetsky Soyuz on the line. She wants to talk to you.
Soyuz? What's she doing in the Pacific?
Patch me through to her.
It's a relief to see you're okay, Comrade Commander.
I wasn't expecting us to meet again under these circumstances, but here we are.
What are you doing all the way out here? Did something happen back home?
Well, I'm sure you're already aware of the illusion that has struck Europa.
All across Europa, regions of space capable of causing widespread, mass delusions have appeared.
These spaces are growing in size, but fortunately, those who've snapped out of the illusion are able to emerge by themselves, to an extent.
However, since their growth cannot be stopped, we've relocated our chain of command to this side of the planet.
We've issued commands for all our fleets to relocate as well, but we were slightly too late. Only approximately 50% of our forces made the move in time.
Luckily, some of our assets and shipgirls are outside the range of these spaces – those being our Arctic fleet, blueprint ships, and our research teams.
On the whole, I would argue the Northern Parliament still has the crisis under control.
Rest assured, we will mount a counterattack as soon as our investigation into these spaces is complete.
Additionally, I have something else to relay to you.
Yat Sen told me that you want to know what's happening across the Pacific.
The Northern Parliament is willing to share what information it has.
According to our defense fleet stationed on the Aleutian Islands, there is currently fighting taking place all across the Pacific.
The Sakura Empire is on the offensive, while the Eagle Union has a fleet moving toward the Marshall Islands.
Our intel also claims that the Sakurans have already entered battle on the Solomon Islands and the Marshall Islands.
As you can imagine, it's not so straightforward.
Meanwhile, in the Eagle Union, all contact was lost with DC and the bases at SD and NY at nearly the same time as the illusory spaces appeared.
Considering what's happening in Europa, it's safe to assume the Eagle Union has been similarly affected by illusions.
I'm afraid I can't send forces directly to the Pacific to verify this information since the situation is complicated as it is.
Still, I hope this intel may be of some use to you.
Thanks, Soyuz. It helps a lot.
You're welcome. I hope your operation will be a success.
I hang up the line and start processing the information she just gave me.
(She said it's under control, but it's clear they're in crisis mode as much as the rest of us.)
(Still, it's good to know that they're more or less okay.)
(On the Eagle Union side, I know Enterprise and her peers are out in the Pacific. I know they'll pull through just fine.)
(Based on the last report I got from Europa, Shinano and Clemenceau are trying to create a conceptual weapon to dispel the illusions.)
(While it likely won't solve this crisis in one fell swoop, it can benefit Soyuz if it turns out she needs assistance.)
(Then there's the Sakura Empire... I haven't really gotten any intel on them.)
(They'll have to wait, though. Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon comes first.)
(The Sirens must have noticed that we're gearing up. The fighting is going to be fierce.)
(I'll focus on this operation for now, then take things one at a time.)

En Route to the Solomons.say: The port of PH was back in business, busier than ever before.
In addition to Enterprise's Pacific Fleet, reinforcements had arrived from all over, including the base at SD and Panama Canal.
They had assembled in the briefing room to analyze the current state of the battle.
All of our bases across the Pacific have been recaptured, including PH, AF, and the Panama Canal.
While there have yet to be any casualties due to the illusions, there have been numerous cases of equipment damage.
Lastly, all garrisoned fleets are currently undergoing necessary repairs and rearming so they can join us on the front lines.
You recaptured all these bases in this little time? You really pulled off a miracle.
Yeah. I was expecting this to turn into a battle of attrition.
We couldn't have done it so fast without the talismans the Sakurans gave us – and Fargo's ingenuity.
Oh, speaking of the talismans, I have something to report.
I took the talismans Yorktown's fleet retrieved at SD and sent them over to the Sea of Stars.
Incidentally, they've resumed regular operations and are investigating ways to analyze and break the illusions.
If they're successful, we should be able to liberate the places across the country that have fallen under its influence.
Good job, Saratoga. I knew I could count on you.
Heheh! Of course you can!
We'll handle the domestic situation so you all can focus on the front lines. Anyway, uh, that's all I wanted to say. Back to you.
The situation on the front lines isn't great. The enemy is applying pressure on us, even if they're advancing at a snail's pace.
Their forces are coming from the direction of the Marshall Islands, and their fleets are growing in size.
At present, we count a total of seven of them, each with a presumed flagship with a total hull length of almost three kilometers.
THREE whole kilometers? I haven't even seen a Siren ship that gargantuan before.
Me neither. Worse yet, these ships are equipped with shields and quite good firepower.
Numerous attempts were made to bombard them from the air, but honestly, it had no effect.
We're trying a different approach now – attack with submarines to kill their command unit and send them into disarray.
Wait! Bunker Hill, put that attack on hold!
Our analysis of the enemy just yielded its initial results.
Apparently, what we're up against is a kind of corrosion entity.
As for what kind, they partly base themselves off of existing ships in our world, as opposed to being projected into our branch from another.
That's the reason why we were able to partly identify them unlike all others before. It's also why our attacks are more effective against them.
We still don't know HOW they come into existence, though.
What's clear is that, whoever's masterminding this attack, they've reappropriated Sakuran mass-production tech.
Oh, one more thing...
In all our previous encounters, the corrosion entities vanished into thin air when they were destroyed, leaving not a single trace.
These, on the other hand, leave behind metal parts that resemble some sort of mineral ore.
We analyzed a sample and discovered that these parts possess properties that make them corrode other materials.
The good news is, they can't corrode our hulls. They CAN corrode electronics, though.
Which brings me to why you shouldn't attack that flagship...
We haven't analyzed a sample from it yet, but given how much larger it is than its peers, its corrosion properties might also be on a different level.
Meaning, attacking it may create a wreck that corrodes the surrounding water or gets stuck to the submarines attacking it. It's just speculation, but it's better to be safe than sorry.
Thanks for all that intel. We'll reconsider the attack.
I've got some intel from our observation station in Samoa.
The readings say a fleet has departed from the Solomons and is heading toward the Marshall Islands.
Best-case scenario, the Sakuran fleet stationed there is still affected by the illusion, but they're alive.
Worst-case scenario... they snapped out of it and the enemy wiped them out.
It's all thanks to Shimanto and Harutsuki that we could wrestle back control so quickly.
If they're in danger, we can't just leave them to their fates.
Of course not! Besides, if the Solomons fall, that's another angle for the enemy to attack from!
We're obliged to help them, both from a strategic and a repaying-our-debt-of-kindness angle!
I agree.
Bunker Hill, keep up the defense. Take it slow and steady.
Gather as much intel as you can on the enemy flagships and find out what they're really after.
After you've helped the Sakurans on the Solomons, head for the Marshall Islands from an angle they won't expect.
We'll then pincer the enemy and take them all out.
Roger.
Enterprise, we've still got forces to spare. Should I reroute them to the Solomons?
Those islands will be fine. Send them to Bunker Hill instead.
Our forces can regroup later if saving the Sakurans doesn't prove too costly, and we can expect reinforcements from Iron Blood's Floating Fortress.
If need be, we can always contact Iron Blood directly.
Are you sure that's such a good idea? Wouldn't it be best if we spoke to the Royal Navy fleet stationed in Sydney?
They've got their hands full with tracking Pamiat' Merkuria META and preparing themselves in case something sudden happens in the Antarctic Ocean.
If we draw reinforcements from them, they'll be unable to respond to changes in Antarctica.
Iron Blood is our best bet. The Commander made negotiating with them a lot easier.
Alright. Be careful out there, Enterprise.
You, too.
You have the power of a Type II on your side, but we still haven't figured out who's behind all this. Be ready for anything.
Heehee. I'll be fine. It's true I haven't fought in a long while, but I still remember how to.
This will be my first battle fighting alongside you all since my return to the front lines. Whoever the enemy commander is, they'll regret earning my fury!

Crimson Shadows Fade Not.say: KABOOOM!
With a loud boom, the foes standing in the Sakurans' way were annihilated, leaving only a few wreckages behind.
Lady Nagato, this is the same as our last encounter. Exactly as many enemies from exactly the same direction.
We've been through here several times now. I'm starting to fear we've been thrust behind a different, non-illusory barrier.
If it is a barrier, it must have an exploitable weak point. Shimanto is searching for it.
You must all stay close to me, lest you fall for the illusion's misleadings.
The Sakuran fleet sailed onward through the cherry seas dyed ink black.
(I've undone so many anchors, yet the illusion shows no sign of lifting...)
(In fact, the only effect of undoing these anchors seems to be that the illusion shifts and changes.)
(I'd assumed this illusion was like the rest... In reality, have we walked into a prison?)
(Then there's that Amagi... What if she isn't a mere phantom conjured up by the illusion?)
Nagato racked her brain to recall the crimson figure in a fox mask that passed her by not long ago.
(She said something, but I cannot remember what...)
(I did not listen to her when we first crossed paths, presuming that she was only a figment of this illusion.)
(Yet, I followed her when she walked ahead, as it seemed as though she was guiding me...)
(Despite my misgivings, I did discover those anchors along the way.)
(In hindsight, accompanying her was reckless. I allowed her to lure me...)
(Here, to this sea with the Sacred Sakura...)
(Wait... Sacred Sakura... Amagi?)
(That's it! I understand now!)
(THIS is what Mikasa wanted me to investigate!)
(Amagi's lingering soul swirled around the roots of the Sacred Sakura, allowing her consciousness to take form...)
(Moreover, her range of influence far exceeds the barrier that guards the Sacred Sakura...)
(Ordinarily, such a thing would be impossible... unless someone employed an illusion to extend the Sacred Sakura's domain even beyond the Sakura Islands.)
(But how could anyone do such a thing?)
(I thought that the former Great Sage fixed the seal on the Prime Sakura, then Yamato inherited the Watatsumi...)
(Unless... it actually was not Yamato who took over...)
(Is that it? Is that why she tried to warn me?)
"Heaven's Door will be opened. I beseech you to join us, if possible, Lady Nagato."
"...Lady Nagato."
"Recall Zuikaku posthaste, and keep Shimanto close by your side."
"A fire that will scorch the world is coming."
(A fire that will scorch the world?!)
(How careless I've been... Such a fool I've been!)
(Akagi... Your refusal to let go really is driving you to do unthinkable things.)
(I do empathize. If my sister's hull were damaged and barely able to sustain itself, I would do all in my power to save her.)
(But this? Do you even know who it is that you're trying to revive?)
KABOOOM!
That very moment, a volley of shells struck Nagato's anti-deception barrier.
Explosions went off on the barrier's surface, sending ripples through it.
Urgh!
Lady Nagato!
Son of a... There's no end to them! Where the hell are they coming from?!
Lady Nagato, let me handle this!
You've still yet to fully regain your strength. Pushing yourself is dangerous!
No! I shall do this!
You expended too much of your own strength on creating those talismans. If anyone here needs time to recover, it's you!
That's more than enough reason for us to evacuate immediately, then! Shimanto, have you found a weak spot in the barrier yet?
I have! Come with me!
Halt! Both of you!
Lady Nagato?
This illusion does not exist solely to misguide us. The Sacred Sakura over yonder is its core.
That is the very thing that Mikasa sent us here to investigate!
Change course! We will head to the center of the illusion!

Breaching.say: Having dispatched waves of enemies in their way, Indiana's and Enterprise's fleet then created a torii gate and breached the illusion.
The illusion seen from the inside was like a sprawling forest of cherry blossoms, with a lone, giant, withered tree towering in the distance.
You know, flowers are a symbol of life...
And yet despite how many there are in here, it just feels so desolate.
Is this really it? The illusion that's trapped our Sakuran friends?
I see signs of fighting. They look fresh, too.
Enterprise, you see anything from up above?
More signs of fighting. Loads, even.
That suggests the Sakurans are not just alive, but fighting right this moment.
Doesn't anyone else find it strange that they're still trapped in here?
The Sakurans came to US first, so shouldn't they be able to leave on their own?
This illusion may be unique – possibly even designed specifically to contain them. Whoever's behind all this is targeting them.
Targeting them specifically? That sounds bad! Like, REALLY bad for them!
Found them. They're heading toward that massive tree in the middle.
Then there's still time! We need to hurry!

Ink Upon the New Winds.say: Nagato and her fleet pushed onward, maintaining her protective barrier as they sailed for the Sacred Sakura.
They encountered hardly any opposition along the way, in a clear contrast to when they were trying to break out.
(Why the lack of resistance? Are they trying to bring me closer to the Sacred Sakura?)
(For what reason could that be?)
Unlike the blooming trees all around them, the Sacred Sakura had long since withered.
Nagato gazed up at its branches.
A moment passed. Then, a single red dot appeared on a branch, and suddenly, all the buds blossomed at once.
Lady Nagato...
Lady Nagato?
The clouds parted, and radiant sunlight shone down upon the forest of cherry blossoms.
A gentle voice called out to Nagato again and again. Finally, she came back to her senses.
Amagi? But... Where am I?
She looked around. She was in a guest room, both strangely familiar to her and extravagant in design.
You've seemed a bit distracted ever since you came here.
Is there something on your mind?
Aye... It feels as though I've had a long dream, and I'm dazed after being so suddenly thrust back to reality.
I understand. It's been quite rainy as of late, and anyone would be surprised to see the skies clear out of nowhere.
I know you've been pushing yourself, too. Please take better care of yourself, okay?
Very well. Incidentally...
Was it you who guided me to the Sacred Sakura?
Pardon? Yes, that was me. Why do you ask? Did you have an unpleasant dream?
Dreams are just that – dreams.
Worry not about me. Now, what would you request of me?
If you pardon my rudeness, I would like to make a request on behalf of Kaga.
Very well. Speak.
As you know, the treaty allows any two large capital ships to be converted to aircraft carriers. I seek to rescind the decommissioning of Battleship Kaga based upon the Treaty's demands.
Hmm... Amagi, nobody wishes for this outcome...
The Treaty is binding once signed, I know. There is no changing fate.
Unless someone takes her place...
From the very beginning, the world has never judged who is weak or strong. You are just using that as an excuse to escape from reality.
Lady Nagato, you should already know that I am not fit to serve as the flagship of the Combined Fleet. Sooner or later, the role must be entrusted to someone else.
While she may not be up to the task just yet, if you were to pass on the role to Kaga, I would be able to rest easy knowing that I was able to serve the Sakura Empire, even with this flawed body.
Is this truly what you desire?
Without a doubt. Right now, Kaga is alone in the Mirror Sea due to her despair, but I will personally bring her back.
Should we be able to return safely, I humbly request that you consider my decision, and hold another wargame to see that Kaga can become an aircraft carrier.
Lady Nagato?
I shan't allow it.
Don't give up now, Amagi. I will fix you, I swear it!
"Fix" me? How will you do that?
By using the Watatsumi as well as the Sacred Sakura's power.
Though your hull may degrade, your soul – the thing that makes you who you are – can be sustained with the Sacred Sakura.
The Sacred Sakura will be nourished by the Watatsumi's power, and its roots can be connected to the Prime Sakura.
We can create for you a new body, using the Watatsumi as the core.
I see your plan... It's exactly the same as hers, isn't it?
"Hers"? Whose?
...I see now. What I thought was a dream was reality. THIS is the dream.
From the very beginning, the world has never judged who is weak or strong. You are just using that as an excuse to escape from reality.
The crimson figure did not answer. Wordlessly, she placed her fox mask upon her face.
Shikigami serpent, break this illusion!
The silence between them was broken by a shout from afar.
It seems our conversation is over.
Lady Nagato, please, turn back. Stay out of this matter.
With the illusion broken, foes lying in ambush revealed themselves.
They posed little threat to the combined might of the Sakura Empire's and Eagle Union's fleet, however.
Amidst the chaos, the crimson figure once more disappeared.
All that remained – an echo of her hollow sigh in Nagato's ear.
Solomon Islands - Sakura Empire Base
(THAT'S Nagato? Wow, she's pretty dang cute.)
Hey, are you the leader of this fleet?
I am Nagato, the guardian fox as well as the former flagship of the Sakura Empire's Combined Fleet.
Oops, sorry if that sounded rude. I've heard a lot about you, but I didn't think you'd be so... small.
How dare you?!
Ahem. Pittsburgh.
Right, yeah, I'm going too far. Forgive me.
I'm Enterprise. Flagship of the Eagle Union's Pacific Fleet and currently in charge of operations in and around the Pacific.
It's an honor to meet you, guardian fox.
And so, the Eagle Union and the Sakura Empire finally met on the Solomon Islands.
With a firm handshake, the Pacific's two most influential factions officially made contact.
Enterprise. The Grey Ghost. I've heard about you.
You came to our aid today, and for that, I thank you.
Please, I should be thanking YOU. Your actions saved us.
On behalf of us all... Actually, time is of the essence, so pardon me for cutting to the chase.
According to our intel, the situation is even worse on the Marshall Islands than it is here.
We're going to link up with a capital fleet and launch a pincer attack. Our goal is to break the illusion and help the fleet garrisoned on the island.
Would you be willing to share what your fleet plans to do next?
(The times are changing...)
(To guard and follow tradition, or embark with the new winds... The choice is already clear to me.)
(Much have I experienced since my awakening under the Sacred Sakura, from meeting the Reborn Combined Fleet, to Akagi and her fierce stubbornness.)
Let me preface by saying that all of this – these illusions, these strange new foes – it all has to do with a plan that Akagi advocates for.
She has taken the Watatsumi and the Sacred Sakura and repurposed them to her own malicious ends. I can tolerate it no longer.
The Sakura Empire is ultimately responsible for this crisis, and you were caught in the middle of it.
For that, I will bear responsibility. My fleet will gladly accompany you and crush this scourge upon our seas.
Once that is done, we'll have to see what comes next.
I couldn't ask for more. I'm just glad I won't need to explain later why our forces were seen within earshot of the Marshall Islands.
With your help, I'm confident this operation will go without a hitch.
Aye. Let us form up and disembark for the Marshall Islands!

Plan B.say: From atop her high tower, the true ruler of the Pacific observed the battlefield through her many hidden cameras.
Her gaze slid past the countless lights over the West Pacific and stopped on the East Pacific – on the Dragon Empery.
The situation is deteriorating further.
A staggering amount of the Empery's electricity has been rerouted to one location – the Celestial Tower.
Early observations indicate it's more than 40% of all their electricity, and the number is growing.
I can also see that the whole country has entered level one combat readiness status.
Considering the size of their force, and the fact that the Commander is at the helm, there's a 98% probability that they'll breach through the Storm.
In conclusion, emergency measures must be taken.
Observer's orders are clear – any attempts to destroy the Storm must be stopped until the experiment reaches its next phase.
As a precautionary measure, I'm raising the Storm's intensity from IV to V.
I'll hold them back until they run out of ammunition and can't keep up the assault any longer.
Tester came to a conclusion and began sending out orders across all her modules.
The modules, however, did not respond to the orders. They remained unresponsive even after seconds passed.
Did my connection time out?
Is somebody jamming the tower of my mainframe?
No... That's impossible...
In the city of steel that is her mainframe, a single blue dot flickered.
The flicker burst into a dazzling torrent that spread across the entire city.
It faded, becoming a dot once more before disappearing right where it originated.
Orders received.
Canceling original plan to increase Storm's intensity.
Alternate plan received.
Activating Type IV mass-produced ships.
Repositioning units to form defensive line. Engaging enemy units until Storm initiation sequence has completed.
She issued new sets of orders to her many modules, and this time, they responded.
With a series of electrical signals, construction was begun on the Type IV mass-produced model – massive units that have rarely been seen in test site beta.
The situation was now a powder keg waiting to blow.

sequence: Before Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon's Start

Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon.say: Together as one! Advance with courage!
The final victory will be ours!
Yat Sen delivered one last rousing speech.
All the communication lines were dead silent. Every fleet was mustering their courage and waiting for the shot to begin the operation.
Storm Alpha – all monitoring values normal. Storms Beta through Delta – all normal.
Final check complete. The four principal Storm monitoring values are all within normal ranges.
Hooray! Victory is guaranteed for us as long as the Storm doesn't ramp up!
Ahem.
Right, uhh... All units, in position!
853 Dongyu-type arsenal vessels are standing by on the front lines, awaiting orders.
Supply and cargo vessels are also ready to go.
It's estimated that our supplies are sufficient to enable five rounds of saturation attacks, ensuring continuous fleet activity for the next three days.
We've really pulled all the stops for this operation, heheh. The fighters on the front lines are gonna have a heck of a time.
You could say that again.
(These Dongyu-type ships are equipped with their signature Freezing Rain extended-range cluster rockets.)
(They can be launched en masse in little time, and their payload has a potent temperature-lowing effect on impact.)
(All we need to do is destabilize the Storm's thermodynamic equilibrium and we'll win.)
If our simulations are accurate, four rounds of saturation attacks will be enough to completely destroy the four main storms.
With those four gone, the Storm will no longer stand between us and the sea.
The only real uncertainty is what is happening beyond the Storm.
We do know that Siren activity has increased around the Mariana Islands, thanks to the Northern Parliament's intel.
Some have speculated that their stronghold in that area is, in fact, the source of the Storm.
That theory is all but confirmed now that they've begun amassing at the same time as we did.
Then we'll just crush them, too!
That will have to come after we take care of the Storm.
Yes. Taking out the Storm comes first.
Once we do, the Sirens will have a massive army waiting for us. A battle with them is inevitable.
Unfortunately, we'll have to deal with them alone as well. The Northern Parliament can't provide any support.
We will just have to do our best and wait. Comrades, get ready!
Ships and shipgirls of the Dragon Empery were lined up at the front lines, waiting for the signal to begin.
My gosh. I didn't think they were dead serious about taking on the Storm head-on.
Their simulations WERE accurate... but I just can't shake this nervousness inside of me.
It'll be fine. They've prepared for everything. They just have to follow the first step of the plan.
It's only after that when things get dicey. We have very limited intel on how the Sirens in the Pacific will respond.
I think we should expect a fierce battle.
Agreed. All units, begin the countdown!
Eight... Seven... Six... Five...
Four... Three... Two... One...
Commence Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon!
One moment, nothing. The next, hundreds of Dongyu vessels launched their cluster rockets, lighting up the stormy skies.
The Storm trembled under the immense bombardment. It looked like it was even shrinking.
Through the Storm the ice dragon broke, soaring through the air like a rainbow.

Breakthrough.say: Commander, this is the South Sea Storm Analysis Fleet. First round of saturation attack is complete. Target still intact!
This is the North Sea Storm Analysis Fleet, reporting identical results. First round of attack is finished, but target shows no sign of weakening.
East Sea Storm Analysis Fleet here. Completed first round of saturation attack. It did nothing.
That was all our first attacks. Storms Alpha through Delta are all unscathed. Fleets will reload and resupply then initiate second round of attacks.
Understood. When that's done, commence the second round on my command.
Roger that. Will begin second round following resupply.
(This is about what I expected from the first round.)
(It didn't look like it did anything, but the third round should do it. Or the fourth at most.)
The second round of attacks came. While the Storm itself looked unchanged, the abnormal ambient weather blanketing the area clearly let up somewhat.
Then came the third round. Storms Alpha and Delta, which were already comparatively weak, were struggling even to sustain themselves. Meanwhile, storms Beta and Gamma were starting to weaken.
Finally, the fourth round finished. The seas had turned silent.
The Storm was fading away before everyone's eyes while the unusual weather above, too, cleared.
We did it... We defeated the Storm!
Commander, approaching Sirens spotted! They're coming toward us!
With the Storm gone, their jamming should be a lot weaker! Send out scouts, recon from the air, and commence a wide-area radar sweep!
On it! I've heard a lot of stories about what you're capable of, Commander!
Let's send these Sirens back where they came from!

To the Marianas.say: Commander, this is the North Sea Storm Analysis Fleet. All Sirens have been eliminated.
We haven't sighted any elites, so it's likely the force they sent at us is only a scouting fleet.
South Sea Storm Analysis Fleet here. We suspect the same.
All targets have been wiped out. Commencing refuel and resupply.
Great work, both of you. Chi An, what are your damages?
Thanks to the support we received, we only lost less than five percent of combat effectiveness.
Less than three percent here, on account of us being in close proximity to the BP base.
Oh dear... Ours is estimated at around nine percent. That's quite a lot.
It's fine. Your fleet has less firepower than the other two and you have a wider area to cover. You're doing great.
Our max allowable losses for the first stage was 15% total, and you all kept it below that value. Outstanding work.
Huan Ch'ang, do you see any enemy movements within your recon range?
From what I have now, it looks like a sizable force is gathering at their Mariana base. On the bright side, I don't see any reinforcements anywhere else.
In short, there have been no major changes.
The Storm has been eliminated, our damages are within estimates, and there are no surprises from the Sirens. All in all, this is going great.
Yat Sen, do you have anything to report?
Everything is as normal around the BP base. I think we can move on to the next stage of the operation.
Thanks. All fleets, you'll set off in one hour as planned.
Oh, and send the Northern Parliament a message for me – "We'll meet at the Mariana Islands."

I Have a Plan.say: KABOOOM!
Commander, we're losing mass-produced ships at an increasing rate.
The Sirens have deployed Type IVs. Here, we caught a picture of one.
It's half-submerged, but it's possible it's been there for quite a while.
Wait, Type IV?
(I know that one. It's a rare model that was mentioned in Bismarck's files and showed up during Operation Frostfall.)
(This is more of them than we've ever seen before.)
(Chkalov told me they're a big upgrade to the previous model, as you'd expect.)
(The fact they're attacking in such large numbers only makes them even more dangerous.)
(The Empery is heavily reliant on its mass-produced ships, owing to the fact that they have few shipgirls.)
(While our mass-produced ships can handle their Type IIIs, Type IVs are a much bigger ask.)
(We'll be forced into a dragged-out losing battle if we keep fighting like this.)
(Our best hope is to regroup with the Northern Parliament ASAP.)
BEEP BEEP!
Commander, they've sent in Type IVs. Our mass-produced ships are taking heavy casualties.
I have a plan, though. We'll bombard the enemy using our Dongyus.
The Type IVs may be powerful, but even they can't survive a volley of cluster rockets!
Are you sure that's going to work?
I'm sure! We gathered intel on the Type IV and ran combat simulations back at the base to test the theory.
Trust me! It's going to work!

Freedom and the Future.say: You're pushing your luck, shipgirls of the Dragon Empery.
Cease fighting and return to your base and I'll leave you alone until the Storm is operational again.
Or, you can stay and I'll sink every last one of you.
Hah! You expect us to accept being stuck on land 'cause of the Storm?
Imagine thinking we'll just lie down and take it.
You know you can't defeat us, so now you're trying to buy time for yourself!

options.content: (Turn her threat right back at her.)
(Tell her nobody is sinking on your watch.)

Freedom and the Future.say: Yeah! Get her ass!
That's not going to happen. An Shan, tell it to her.
If it's a fight you want then come at us! Alternatively, surrender peacefully and we'll let you live!
Oh yeah? I'll let you go if you run back to your Mariana Islands stronghold. Or, you can stay...
And we'll send you to the bottom of the sea!
I'm the Commander of the Azur Lane. I've fought countless battles across the globe, and protected every one of the shipgirls under my command every time.
This battle will be no exception.
So much for talking it out. Ships, advance!
This is your final warning. Take your fleet and retreat now.
KABOOOM!
Did ya hear that? That was our final answer!
What's the matter? Cat got your tongue?
BEEP BEEP!
Commander, numerous Tester vessels have been spotted on our course.
Can you defeat them?
Easily.
We're seeing Tester vessels here as well. There's a dozen or more of them!
A dozen is a lot... Can you can handle them?
We will. Our fleet will accomplish its objective no matter what.
Alright. I'll apply enough pressure to deal with Tester herself to make sure she can't divert resources to her vessels.
Continue the operation. We'll rendezvous at the Mariana stronghold!
KABOOOM!
Through teamwork and the Dongyus' rocket barrages, we managed to endure Tester's assault.
Her vessels went down one after the next. Our victory is all but certain.
You broke through the Storm, clueless as to what lies beyond it.
Hell, you don't even know we're trying to protect you.
Even if we assume you're telling the truth, your "protection" is no better than a cage.
Besides, we can clearly see what lies beyond the storm.
It is freedom. Freedom and the future!
I will not let you go there.
Too late. We're already going there!
Destroying your Storm was only the first step! We're gonna choose our own path!
If you think you can stop us then quit yapping and try it!
We'll crush your fleet, your vessels, AND your mainframe!
KABOOOM!
After much fierce fighting, all of Tester's vessels have been destroyed.
BEEP BEEP!
Commander, this is the East Storm Analysis Fleet. All Sirens have been eliminated.
We've broken through the storm area and are currently en route to the destination!
So am I. Heading to the objective now.
We're on the way, too!
Reports are coming in from all directions that Tester's vessels have been taken care of.
At long last, the Dragon Empery has broken through the Storm and set forth toward their own future.

Concurrent Operations.say: There are three steps to Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon, each with their own objectives.
The first – break through the storm and eliminate all Sirens in the area. When this step has been completed, and only if losses are less than 15%, can step two be initiated.
Next step – advance to the Mariana Islands stronghold and regroup with the Northern Parliament's support fleet.
Finally – eliminate the Siren presence at the stronghold and neutralize all the facilities on the island.
Now that it's all but certain that the Mariana Islands are the origin point of the Storm, we know that we must root them out entirely, lest all our efforts be for nothing.
After savoring the glory of eliminating Tester for just a moment, all our fleets focused their minds once more on the main goal.
All three of us coming from a different direction, we reunite in the waters of the Mariana Islands.
Commander, I've received a report of what's been happening in the Pacific.
The Sea of Stars has widely deployed drones with signal enhancement functions across various bases.
This will facilitate much smoother communication between our units, moving forward.
(I've dealt with mass illusions before, but never on THIS scale.)
(It's affecting not just the Pacific, but the Eagle Union and Europa as well.)
(A remote base like PH falling into enemy hands would be bad enough, but NY City and the Sea of Stars almost fell, too.)
(Thankfully, Shimanto and Harutsuki came to the rescue, and the hard work of Saratoga, Indiana, and Enterprise pulled us back from the edge of the cliff.)
(Which brings us to where we are now...)
(All illusory realms detected across the Eagle Union have been erased.)
(Enterprise has grouped up with Nagato's fleet and they're all en route to the Marshall Islands.)
(It's not easy out there, and the fact they're working together brings a smile to my face.)
(I wouldn't have expected the guardian fox to appear on the front lines of such an unusual operation, but any help we can get is appreciated.)
(If we can get her on our side, reforming the Azur Lane should be easier.)
(Then there's that report she gave me. About Akagi...)
(And her alleged connection to this whole incident.)
(I don't know how to feel about Akagi being involved in this.)
(We once fought, she and I. Right here, in the Pacific.)
(It was a sudden attack. Nobody anticipated it.)
(Then came the battle of the Coral Sea.)
(And then the battle at AF.)
(There were rumors that she'd sunk following that battle. It was later proven to be false.)
(She's a crafty one, that fox. Leading the Sakura Empire by day, and working out schemes by night. I wish I'd had more chances to speak to her one-on-one.)
(We DID fight alongside rather than against each other during the battle of Compiler's mainframe...)
(That was a time for action, not for dialogue, though. A shame.)
(Maybe I should've pulled her aside and talked to her...)
(While she didn't attend the meetings to discuss the Azur Lane's reformation, I've heard rumors that she has a rather distinct stance on the topic.)
(Still, something doesn't sit right with me.)
(Even if she does have a dangerous side to her, she doesn't strike me as someone who'd cause an incident that has world-spanning effects.)
(After all we've been through – after all SHE'S been through – I don't want to believe she'd throw her friends under the bus like this.)
(She's not some egomaniac who wants to take over the world...)
(...Or maybe I don't know her as well as I think I do.)
(I'll task some people with gathering intel for me, just to prepare for a possible worst-case scenario.)
(For now, I need to focus on what's right in front of me – recapturing the Marshall Islands and toppling the Mariana Islands stronghold.)
(Starting with the Marshall Islands. It's fallen into the hands of that still-unidentified enemy, who's using it as a forward operating base.)
(Bunker Hill's fleet is blockading the path to other bases. They'll make sure nothing comes in or out.)
(Enterprise, Nagato, and their fleets have departed from the Solomons. They'll soon reach the Marshall Islands.)
(Once they're there, Bunker Hill will join them in recapturing the island.)
(For backup, we have two Eagle Union fleets in reserve.)
(One will set off from SD, resupply and refuel at PH, then head straight to us.)
(The other comes from NY, which does mean they'll have to take the route through Panama Canal.)
(They're really far away and won't make it in time, but I'm bringing them over just in case anyway.)
(Then there's my current objective – the Mariana Islands.)
(For the longest time, it had been a very unremarkable base for the Sirens with little activity to speak of.)
(That is, until last year when the Dragon Empery began evaluating a plan to break through the Storm. Suddenly, it flared up with activity.)
(It's almost like the Sirens knew what the Empery was planning, just like how the Storm intensified when the Empery rallied its troops.)
(That was essentially soft confirmation that the Storm's control center is on those islands.)
(This theory was further reinforced by the intel Iron Blood shared, as well as Tester's serious attempt to push us back.)
(Boy, did all of this come at the perfect time for us.)
(The Empery is sending in fleets from three directions, and the Northern Parliament is sending a fleet of their own to the south.)
(Seeing as we've worked around the jamming, maybe I should also ask for help from the garrisoned fleet at Iron Blood's Floating Fortress.)
(Oh, and then there's the Royal Navy fleet stationed in Sydney.)
(From what I've heard from our allies at Tierra del Fuego, there's not much of anything happening around Antarctica. Not yet, anyway.)
(On one hand, someone has to keep an eye on Pamiat' Merkuria META. On the other, spreading my forces isn't ideal.)
(I don't like having to choose one over the other, but...)
(Alright. Maybe something like...)
(I'll tell the Royal Navy's fleet to join in the attack on the Mariana Islands.)
(Pamiat' likely won't be a problem. Kimberly said she simply wanted to enjoy a vacation for some reason. Besides, I can count on Observer not letting Pamiat' do as she pleases in Antarctica.)
(As the old saying goes – the enemy of my enemy is my friend.)
(Finally, we have the question of how to allocate forces for these two separate operations.)
(The Marshall Islands are the unknown enemy's headquarters, and they're still affected by the illusions. They're a threat we need to take seriously.)
(At the same time, the Storm can return if we don't destroy the control center soon. We need to be ready for that.)
(The Empery is keeping the storm in check with their Dongyus, but they can't keep up the assault forever.)
(There's also very little time to reposition anyone since the Mariana Islands will keep producing those Type IVs.)
(Okay. I've got it.)
(We'll just run both operations concurrently.)
(Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon will continue as planned. If it feels like we're outnumbered, I'll wait for backup from the Royal Navy and Iron Blood.)
(If Enterprise, Indiana, and Nagato take back the Marshall Islands quick enough, I'll regroup with them, too.)
(So that's it. Destroy the Storm's control center and neutralize the Type IV production facilities.)
(As nice as it would be to destroy a mainframe today, there's no way Tester will expose her beating heart to us like that.)
(I also don't want to provoke her. Playing safe is the name of the game here.)
Memphis, I've got some orders.
I'm listening. Whenever you're ready.
Thanks. Be sure to write this down.
Of course!
One – tell the bases at Karaginsky, BP, and SD to keep close eyes on the Sakura Islands. Gather intel whenever possible.
Two – tell the Royal Navy fleet stationed in Sydney to depart for the Mariana Islands and aid in the operation to capture it.
Additionally, they must be ready to relay any monitoring info from Antarctica and its ocean directly to me.
Three – tell the Eagle Union fleet stationed at Tierra del Fuego to be on standby. Command of it will be transferred to the Royal Navy's fleet in Sydney until this operation has concluded.
Four – contact Peter Strasser and inform her that we request the Floating Fortress Fleet's assistance in the capture of the Mariana Islands.
Additionally, share with her all the intel relevant to this operation and tell her to contact her comrades in Europa immediately to let them know.
Five – inform Enterprise that her group is responsible for recapturing the Marshall Islands.
Their mission continues as planned under her leadership. The Sakuran fleet accompanying her is to follow her orders.
Six – Operation Wind-Crushing Ice Dragon will continue as planned. The Northern Parliament's and Dragon Empery's fleets are to aid in destroying the Sirens' stronghold on the Mariana Islands.
Finally, seven – if one of our two task forces finishes their objective before the other does, they are to swiftly link up with the other.
Got it.
Is there anything else?
No, that's everything. I'm going to rest my eyes a bit now. If something happens, give me a call.
Heehee. You have a nice, long rest now.
You're really going above and beyond for us all here, Commander. Thank you.
Don't mention it. I'm just doing my job.

I Am Amagi.say: The Marshall Islands stood ominously still on the horizon, juxtaposed by a trio of approaching fleets.
Following their battle at the Solomon Islands, the shipgirls determined that the illusion gripping the Marshall Islands was far, far more dangerous than anticipated.
Enterprise, Nagato, and her fleet got into attacking positions while Bunker Hill pivoted from defense to offense, trying to draw the enemy's attention as much as possible.
Meanwhile, Enterprise's fleet split up into smaller squadrons that each attacked one island, probing around to try to find the heart of the illusion.
Birds were singing, flowers were blooming, and blue waters intersected green mountains. It was like a painting, inviting you to get lost inside it.
Man. This place sure is a sight for sore eyes.
It's so beautiful – like a dream come true. Too beautiful to be real.
Lady Nagato, do you recognize this?
Aye. It's unmistakeable.
Nagato surveyed her surroundings, a grim look occupying her face.
Do you know what this place is?
It is the sanctuary of the Prime Sakura.
It is a closely guarded place, thus it's to be expected that none of you know it.
(If the Eagle Union ladies' speculation is true, then the illusion is a projection of the dreams of those within it.)
(Supposing that the Solomon Islands' illusion was a projection of mine, then whose is this?)
(Unzen, mayhaps? No, that cannot be. She is under Yamato's protection.)
(Musashi, then? Highly unlikely. She has left the Sakura Islands and would have no reason to come here.)
Lady Nagato, it appears that some people are sleeping up ahead.
Sleeping within a dream, are they? Show me to them.
There was a Sakuran gazebo by the stream. Surrounding it was grass and young girls wearing Sakuran clothing, fast asleep.
What are our submarines doing here? They can't all have been stationed on the Solomons!
Wake up! Open your eyes!
It's no good. They won't awaken.
There is a picnic blanket and snacks strewn around inside the gazebo. I would assume that they were going to have a flower viewing party.
Lady Nagato, there's someone else here! It's Ryuuhou!
What? Ryuuhou?
(I could've sworn she was in the Diadem of Light... If not there, then in Europa with Suruga and Noshiro.)
(Did Akagi purposely bring her here? If so, why?)
(I take that back... She is clearly under the illusion's spell.)
(Meaningless would it be to place her here to do something only for her to be put to sleep.)
Nagato and the rest marched on, investigating the illusion.
(This is no impostor. She is the real Ryuuhou.)
(How strange. Neither she nor the submarines have ever seen the Prime Sakura's sanctuary.)
(Then, if none of them know how it looks, who in the world could have created this place?)
(Furthermore, if they lost consciousness in the illusion, would it still project what's in their minds?)
(If imprisoning them here is the goal, what reason is there to render them unconscious?)
The Sakurans moved the unconscious girls to a safe place, then began heading toward the illusory Prime Sakura.
After a peaceful walk free of enemy attacks, the girls arrived at the foot of the Prime Sakura.
(This tree is the heart of the illusion – and the reason why the illusion has become so powerful.)
(Within the illusion on the Solomon Islands, I collected a fragment of the Watatsumi.)
(The illusion was just that – an illusion. So was the Prime Sakura. Yet, the Watatsumi fragment was undeniably genuine.)
(In this fragment, I sensed the corruption. Faint, but certain.)
(The Great Sage rid the Watatsumi of corruption, so why has it become tainted once more?)
Nagato placed her hand on the Prime Sakura and sensed the flow of its energy.
(I am certain now. This Prime Sakura is indeed what sustains the illusion.)
(Yet, strangely, its aura passes through the other trees in the illusion and connects to the outside... But why?)
(Oh no... The illusion must be pumping energy into a foe on the outside!)
(It's little wonder she was able to maintain her form, in spite of the Shadow of Corruption...)
Our Eagle Union allies will be overwhelmed soon. We must act quickly.
Shimanto, Kongou, take the others and destroy all the trees. They're sending energy to someone outside the illusion.
I shall dispose of this false Prime Sakura.
After issuing her orders, Nagato focused her mind on the Prime Sakura again.
As she did, her surroundings suddenly changed in the blink of an eye.
(Much like the real Prime Sakura, it has a grotto-heaven inside of it.)
(I was right. The tree's energy is flowing out to the trees, which then reroute it to the outside.)
(One needs to destroy the power source within this grotto to curb the flow. The tree itself will simply regenerate if felled.)
(You sly dog, you...)
I knew you'd come, Lady Nagato.
...Amagi. Is this your doing?
I thought the Watatsumi's corruption had polluted your soul... Yet, on the Solomon Islands, you wanted me to walk away from this incident.
I have many, many questions for you.
The only issue is...
You don't know how much of my true self remains. Is that it?
Heehee. I understand. When we spoke on the Solomons, I'd say about 30% of myself remained.
Now, however... I'm honestly not even sure, myself.
I see...

To Nagato, Amagi was an unfortunate soul born with a defective hull. She was laid to sleep at the Prime Sakura's foot, praying she would recover. Alas, the Watatsumi's impurity took away even her hopes of ever waking again.
Nagato had known this for a long time, but hearing the words straight from Amagi's mouth cast a shadow of sadness over her.
Only 30% of her was still Amagi. Even so, she had to speak her mind to the one who worried for her.
(I won't run away... No longer.)
Amagi... Just who are you?
I'm Amagi.
Are you really?
I am. I have her likeness, memories, personality, way of thinking, even her mannerisms.
Amagi is the only thing I am.
Speak not such absurdities. Amagi would never do what you're doing now.
Correct – I WOULD never do such a thing in the past. However, you're speaking to my future self.
I'm afraid the person you once knew and the person standing here now are different people.
That is a lie.
You are not, and will never be, Amagi.
You are nothing but a doppelganger. A doppelganger who devoured her soul and regrettably succumbed to evil.
And I... I will put a stop to you!
I had a feeling this would happen.
Be that as it may, I must accomplish my objective.
I will not be stopped. Not by you or anyone else.
Not even you – my dear sister Amagi!
A thousand shikigami flew through the air, painting the grotto a pinkish color.
Spells flashed brightly and cannons boomed thunderously. A fierce battle began.

Shadow of Corruption.say: Shikigami serpent, strike like the winter moon!
KABOOOM!
Nagato! Are you okay?!
I knew it! There's a hidden second layer to this damn place!
We more or less dispelled the illusion on every other island, but this one remained for some reason!
Good thing we came to check on y'all! And even better that we're not too late!
Forgive me, Lady Nagato! Breaking down the barrier took a while.
Get behind us, guardian fox!
Well, you got here early.
Forget about me! Bombard that evil spirit with all you have!
She is extremely dangerous! We must eliminate her here and now!
In that brief exchange, Nagato had figured out who their opponent really was.
She sensed in her the same aura as she did from the Watatsumi fragment. The same corruption that rotted the Prime Sakura.
This was not Amagi – it was the Shadow of Corruption.
The corruption that Musashi and the Great Sage worked so hard to purge had not only returned, but also it seemed even stronger than it had been in the sanctuary.
(The Eagle Union is with us! We need only stop this evil spirit, drain her power, and exorcise her one more–)
(No! Damn! She cannot be stopped with ordinary attacks! The power she just flaunted was but a ruse to lure me closer!)
By the time Nagato finally realized she had carelessly reached the trunk of the withered Prime Sakura, it was too late.
The next moment, a massive entity perched atop a dead branch leaped down to attack her.
- No! - LADY NAGATO!
Just before the torrent of black mist could consume her, someone jumped out to protect her.
It was Hiei – or rather, she looked like Hiei. The dark dragon swallowed her instead, then flew skyward.
A moment later, it was all gone. The Amagi impostor, the Shadow of Corruption, and the woman who sacrificed herself for Nagato.

Recapture.say: Sometime later...
Enterprise, I did a sweep of the area, but couldn't find anything.
Then we'll have to expand our search range.
You do that! Me and the other subs will go have a look!
Lady Nagato, I'll search the inside of the illusion one more time.
Let me help you with that!
Enough already. I'm calling an end to the search.
What?! Why?
They aren't here. She used a Sacred Sakura to move to a new domain.
It would be one thing if the Sacred Sakura domain was limited to the Sakura Islands. With it being stretched this wide, though, there's no reason to search this one place from top to bottom.
Wait, so you're saying she kidnapped Hiei and just teleported somewhere?
Aye. Warping with a Sacred Sakura was a closely guarded secret of ours. I needn't withhold that knowledge now that you've witnessed it.
(I swore to protect them. All of them. And yet, my own carelessness led to Hiei being abducted right before my eyes...)
(No, calm down. It will be okay.)
(That Amagi-like spirit was after me. She should be coming back to finish the job...)
(And yet, rather than try again after she got Hiei, she simply vanished and hasn't been back since.)
(This gives me a very, very bad premonition.)
(I need to...)
(No. Calm down. You cannot afford to lose your cool again.)
(For now, it's time to recapture the Marshall Islands and meet up with the Commander!)
Nagato took several deep, calming breaths, then placed her hand on the false Prime Sakura's trunk again.
(There it is. There is another Watatsumi fragment here. I sense corruption, too.)
Crumble to dust.
Its core now pulverized, the Marshall Islands illusion fell apart in an instant.
The black vessels beyond the illusion, too, disintegrated as their power source had been destroyed.
At last, the battle of the Marshall Islands ended in a victory for the shipgirls.

Under Dark Currents.say: Four fleets are displayed on the command system screen – three en route toward the Mariana Islands, and one giant fleet that just recaptured the Marshall Islands.
Thanks to this, the operation is still on track, despite the slow-down.
It's good our fleets advanced carefully, since now that we've retaken the Marshall Islands, we luckily have some troops to spare.
The bad news is, moving forward will only get harder from here on out.
Do you want me to tell the Royal Navy and Iron Blood to pick up the pace?
That won't be necessary. Considering our now-advantageous position, I'd like to keep them in reserve.
You're worried about that Amagi, aren't you?
I am. After hearing the reports of what she did, plus Nagato's account of what happened, I have good reason to be.
We need to keep an eye out for any surprises from her.
(I wonder what she wants – that being who has some traits from both Akagi and Amagi.)
Sometime earlier...
....As you've surmised, it was not the Sirens who were responsible for the illusory phenomena. It was someone who claimed – and believed – to be Amagi.
To me, she is akin to a lingering will given form by the Watatsumi, tainted with corrosion from head to toe.
...Indeed, she shares many similarities with the beings you call corrosion entities.
One could say she is an offshoot of the corruption that eats away at the world. It would lend sense to us calling it "the Shadow of Corruption."
However, what sets the Shadow of Corruption apart is that it took the form of our own mass-produced ships on the battlefield.
Moreover, the Amagi-like entity knew how to and was able to use a Sacred Sakura domain to move to a new location.
...It's true. The Sacred Sakura domain, the Sirens' teleporters, and the microlayer transference system share some striking similarities.
Putting that aside, what's important is that this entity is dangerous.
You and I both have apprehensions about Akagi. It's for that reason we must stop her as soon as possible.
Otherwise, she may un-become herself and turn into a monster that ravages not just the Sakura Empire, but the entire world...
...What? Don't try to be everywhere at once?
...So you are keeping an eye on the Sakura Empire, too. I see.
Then I will take your advice to heart and do what I can. To a reasonable degree.
(I've considered a lot of angles, and none of them quite explain what – or who – this fusion of Amagi and Akagi really is.)

(Is she a part of Amagi's soul which the corruption absorbed? Is she a phantom conjured up by the corruption? Is she a puppet controlled by Akagi? Is she Akagi herself, distorted and warped by the corruption? The possibilities are many.)
(Personally, I doubt that she's either simply a puppet or a corrosion entity with identity issues.)
(What we know for sure is that she's extremely dangerous and not to be taken lightly.)
(...Thanks for this, Nagato. Be safe out there.)

The Forces Gather.say: BEEP BEEP!
Commander, the fleet is in position. I can see the Sirens' defenses from here.
Chen Hai, Hwah Jah, what's your status?
In position, ready and waiting to start firing at your command.
Ditto. We've arrived at our designated location, awaiting your order to open fire.
BEEP BEEP!
This is Sovetsky Soyuz, reporting to the Empery and the Commander. Our Antarctic Fleet and Blueprint Fleet are in position and awaiting orders.
Are you commanding the fleets yourself?
I am. Our objective, after all, is to take the Mariana Islands and destroy the machine that creates the Storm.
To that end, I feel I should be on the ground with my troops. Well, on the water, but you know the saying.
I have blueprint ships and Kirov META accompanying me. I hope they'll be of service to you.
Admiral Nakhimov here. I'm glad to be fighting with you all.
Bringing several different factions to the same battle... Very interesting. I'll be watching and learning from your commanding style.
BEEP BEEP!
This is Peter Strasser of Iron Blood.
You called, Officer, and the Floating Fortress Fleet has answered. We will help you capture the Mariana Islands.
Hindenburg speaking. We're waiting, Contract Binder. Give us your orders.
Heheh. I could never turn down a request for aid from you.
I expect this to be a rousing battle. As always.
The Royal Navy's South Pacific Fleet has arrived.
We encountered an Orthodoxy squire along the way. She wants to talk to you, so I'll put her on.
I am Forbin, Templar Knight of the Orthodoxy! I'm here to partake in your operation on Cardinal Richelieu's orders!
Wait, Forbin? Are you able to signal Richelieu all the way from here?
Well, I was! I received a very brief transmission from the Cardinal. She said she's dealing with the illusions in Europa as we speak!
(That's good news. Means Shinano and Clemenceau are handling things well. That's one less thing to worry about.)
BEEP BEEP!
This is Nagato. My fleet is in position.
Excellent. Thanks for letting me know.
Hey, Hornet here! With my Type II and all! I'm on the way!
Yorktown's staying at SD, so she handed command over our fleet to me!
Good call. She helps the place run, so it's best that she stays there until all the problems caused by the illusions have been sorted out.
Yeah, that's what she told me! Oh, and that she's sad she didn't get to meet the Commander, but there's always next time!
Man, you three are so close. Commander and Enty and Yorky, sitting in a tree...
Hornet.
Uh-oh! That's my cue to shut up! Sorry!
Ahem. Commander, our fleet will arrive in an hour. Unfortunately, Hornet's fleet will need two hours even at top speed to get here.
We don't wanna give the enemy too much time, so let's launch the attack in an hour. The Eagle Union will lead the charge – the other fleets can follow our trail.
No problem. The attack will start in one hour, then.

We Are Amagi.say: KABOOOM!
Harutsuki! You okay?
I-I'm fine, thank you!
These Type IV Sirens mean business. Don't let your guard down.
Okay! I'll be careful.
Come with me. We're gonna regroup with the Sakurans.
As you say!
Wait! Indiana, behind you!
God dammit! Turn around for one second and they rush you like hyenas!
Think you can catch me off guard?!
KABOOOM!
Another Tester vessel destroyed. One point for me.
Phew. Sure looks like that enemy stronghold is getting cooked.
This'll be done in no time at this rate. Hahah.
Got a report from Chen Hai! They've destroyed what they believe to be the Storm's control device!
Also, the coordinated attack on the stronghold is going well! The mission objective should be completed pretty soon!
Alright! Once the fortress is destroyed, all fleets will pull out of the area. Destroying Tester's mainframe will have to come another time when we're better prepared.
Well, about that mainframe – I think she's exposing it to us.
Sending you the recon data I took. Take a look.
Hornet sends me a picture and a set of coordinates.
The picture depicts a great rupture suspended above the sea, almost like someone punched a giant hole through space.
On the other side of the hole, there are thousands of faintly glowing lines, and in the background, a sprawling city of steel situated in a sea of electronics.
Is that really it? Her mainframe?
She's showing it for the whole world to see?
Invasion prevention plan must be executed... Storm must be reconstructed...
Actual test site values... far exceed projected values...
Requesting local cleanup authorization...
"Cleanup"? That sounds bad!
Very bad. Compiler tried to initiate the same thing when we breached her mainframe.
Our forces are too spread out to take her on. I say we retreat before she can activate it.
Why the hell is she escalating like this?! We aren't even after her damn mainframe!
Maybe it's not us... It's someone else!
REQUEST DENIED.
YOUR VESSEL IS SHOWING SIGNS OF MANIPULATION. SUBMIT TO A SELF-DIAGNOSTIC.
A self-diagnostic NOW?!
I'm perfectly fine! Do I look like I'm malfunctioning?!
Look-look-look-look li-li-like mal-mal-mal-mal–
KABOOOM!
Oh dear. You should see a repairman about that.
From seemingly nowhere, the Amagi doppelganger appeared.
With that, the final Tester vessel on the path toward her mainframe was destroyed instantaneously.
I'll make good use of your mainframe, if you don't mind.
Dark tendrils pulsated forth from her fox mask.
Black mist shot through the air, aiming for the rift in space where the wrecks of Tester and her ships lay.
But, just as they were about to enter the rift, they hit an invisible wall, sending sparks flying.
Meanwhile, more of the black mist had covered the wreckages of the mass-produced ships. It pulled them together, transmutating them into a single, enormous vessel.
BEEP BEEP!
It's her! That's the Shadow of Corruption!
She is trying to seize control of Tester's mainframe and connect the Watatsumi to her false Sacred Sakura!
Commander, you must stop her IMMEDIATELY!
Computational resources. A soul. A body...
Now I have them all.
Orochi! Reveal yourself to me!
The sea was blanketed in the black mist of corrosion. It first became a gust, then a tornado, then assumed its final form as a corrosion entity.
The colossal battleship dubbed "Orochi" radiated a foreboding crimson light, its form distorting.
Then, it shrunk down, taking the form of a human.
First, there was one, then two, then three, ten, a hundred...
Finally, a crimson crowd of people stood there ominously, like an army of Pawns.
Those... things... they're not shipgirls.
Nor are they Pawns, nor are they ships... and they're certainly not people!
This is mad!
What have you done?! You've horribly misused the Watatsumi's power!
Ahahahahaha!
I've done it!
I'm so, so happy!
Cacophonous laughter echoed across the sea.
Abnormal weather detected! Corrosion entities are appearing... and mass-produced ships... My god, there are so many of them!
The impostor's strength is still growing... How? She should have no power outside of an illusion...
The mainframe! That's it! If need be, we must put our lives on the line to stop her from reaching it!
But how? That corrosion storm is too dangerous to get close!
There is a solution! That storm is very similar to the one we destroyed just earlier! What's more, it's already very unstable!
I believe we can dissipate it using the Dongyus' rockets!
Doesn't hurt to try! Do it!
Dragon Empery units, target the corrosion storm with your Dongyus! Iron Blood units, protect them!
Enterprise, Bunker Hill, Pittsburgh, disperse the crowd of Amagi doppelgangers!
We'll head for Tester's mainframe and stop the head Amagi from getting in at any cost!

Another Calamity.say: KABOOOM!
With your blasted corrosion storm gone, you cannot move as you please!
Give up! Your plan has failed!
The Dongyus were successful in eliminating the corrosion storm – the very thing that sustained the army of the Amagi doppelganger. Without it, the Eagle Union readily eliminated them all.
You look so awe-inspiring now, Lady Nagato. I'm surprised.
Do not speak using Amagi's voice, you fiend.
You can think whatever you like, because – guess what? I've already succeeded.
"Succeeded"? You're lying to yourself about being satisfied with creating these abominations.
Do you think THAT'S what Akagi would've wanted?
Not at all. The fates of these creatures were sealed the moment they were made.
Tell me, Nagato – when you look at them, what do you see?
A herd of impostors that shan't ever, ever reach the truth.
Interesting. Impostors of who, exactly?
What do you mean, "who"?
A falsehood can only exist if a truth exists. Imitations owe their existence to an original. Whose hull are they based on, and where is its owner?
The owner is long since dead!
Dead? Is she? Why?
Close your eyes and think about it, please. Have you not found a way to cure Amagi's illness?
What are you talking about? That was a dream – a vision produced by the illusion.
A dream, you say?
You are in reality right now. Yet you cannot forget the memory of that perfect dream you had.
You can't be serious...
Where there is cause, there is effect, and vice versa.
The impostor is the effect, the original is the cause. It's only logical.
No... You implanted memories within us, then used the Watatsumi to make them real... That's it, isn't it? That was your plan all along!
Heehee... AHAHAHAHAHAHA!
The figure with the red fox mask turned around and walked inside the rift in space, laughing triumphantly.
As the last traces of the corrosion storm faded away, the portal to the mainframe closed up.
Dammit! We were THIS close!
No. We were not. That impostor was already in possession of a body able to exist outside an illusion.
She let herself be attacked. She pretended as though she could not reach the mainframe. All to make a mockery of us.
She wanted to flaunt her victory to me...
Commander... I must talk to you...
A crowd of shipgirls had gathered at the entrance to the Mirror Sea housing Tester's mainframe.
The space ahead of them appeared empty. They all knew, of course, that this was only camouflage.
I closely examined this place before you all got here.
The route to the mainframe is undoubtedly here. However, an extremely intricate barrier protects it. It will take some time to dispel it.
A barrier, eh... Hmm...
Given the context, do you think she's talking about a Mirror Sea?
Likely. When Sakurans use the word "barrier," they sometimes refer to a special kind of Mirror Sea known as a locked Mirror Sea.
Locked Mirror Sea? Go on.
Well, the first of its kind was discovered in–
It's a kind of Mirror Sea whose inside is hidden! Ya gotta know the right procedure to actually get inside!
...That's the gist of it.
Yes, that does seem like what this is.
What's that? I think I just heard something interesting!
Our researchers have had success cracking the code to get inside those locked Mirror Seas! This sounds like a good opportunity to test some theories!
What do you say, Commander?
Hmm. The Sea of Stars' computing power would help us find the key much faster.
Perhaps we can help. Our supercomputer – the Celestial Tower – can share the calculation burden.
So we've got a Sakuran expert on the subject, an Empery supercomputer, and our own Sea of Stars...
This should be a piece of cake.
Aye. This will go much quicker if we work together.
Alright, then I'll be the crier!
The what?
Y'know, the one who shouts something when you use a talisman.
I figure we'll need a dedicated crier for this Mirror Sea, so just say the word and I'll be there.
I... What?
Wait, do you NOT need to do that? But Shimanto did it when she threw HER talismans!
That's... simply a habit of mine.
A HABIT?! So I recorded all those cringy lines for nothing?!
Oh god... They've been playing ON LOOP inside so many illusions!
Yeowch. That's embarrassing.
Uuuggghhh! Kill me now!
Hah! Well, let's put that aside and get started.
All fleets, restock and resupply, then prepare to invade the mainframe.
While Tester herself is incapacitated, we should be ready to run into the Amagi doppelganger. Do not let your guard down.
BEEP BEEP BEEP BEEP!
What? An energy pulse?
It looks like it's coming toward us at high speed...
Warning: Massive energy pulse was detected 30 seconds ago, originating from the Sakura Islands.
Energy pulse shares 96.4% similarity with that of the creation of a Singularity.
The size of this Singularity cannot be determined due to all nearby monitoring equipment having gone offline.
The Sea of Stars suggests giving this Singularity the provisional name "Naraka."
Based on calculations using available data, Naraka is believed to affect the entirety of the Sakura Islands.
Threat level: Cataclysmic.
WHAT?!

sequence: The tables were completely turned as one side was on the cusp of victory.
The players moved their pawns while the spectators stood on the battlefield.
Be cautious at every step and plan for everything before you act.
Know yourself and your enemy and you needn't fear the result of a hundred battles.
The battle being waged by every faction goes on.
Whilst the echo from across the starry seas whispers into their ears.

Her Majesty Arrives.say: The streets were abuzz with people. It was another ordinary, peaceful day.
Hey, does anyone else think this vacation has lasted ridiculously long?
What's so bad about that? No orders to return home means endless vacation!
Yeah! I mean, who DOESN'T like a long vacation?
Did you spot a fancy cloud or– Oh my gosh! Girls, look!
A black sun, quietly burning in the dusky sky.
This is your queen speaking! My loyal subjects – wake up!
You're in a sweet but deadly trap, so snap out of it!
Y-Your Majesty, why are you in the sky?
THAT'S the first thing on your mind?
So this whole vacation was nothing but a dream... I can hardly believe how realistic it seemed.
Because it's not entirely a dream. Dreams are natural, whilst yours was fabricated!
It'll be alright, though. This newly developed anti-conceptual weapon will tear down this illusion in only three minutes.
If you feel dizzy or have a headache, that's fine. It'll be gone when you open your eyes.
I'm off to the next illusion now. You lot just focus on waking up!
Let's hope the girls manning the gate haven't done anything stupid...

Untarnished Glory.say: Sardegna Empire - Taranto
Blue skies, blue seas, and sparkling waves as far as the eye could see. It was another ordinary, peaceful day.
Mmmh. This weather is so wonderful.
It makes me want to sail out and practice formations with my aircraft. Will any of you come to watch?
Allow me to accompany you as your escort!
Yeah, I'll come along! I've been meaning to test out the new glider I invented.
Hehehehe... You girls go and enjoy yourself... I'll stay right here...
Sure, we'll– Uh, what's that in the sky?
A black sun, quietly burning in the dusky sky.
Ahem. Veneto here. Can you hear me? I'm speaking to you through unorthodox means.
A black sun... I remember that! It's that conceptual weapon Marco Polo used!
That thing?! Oh no... Don't tell me someone's trying to cause another superimposition event!
Clearly, this is not who we think it is, then!
You! You're merely an impostor claiming to be Veneto! Who are you really, and what do you want?
Huh?! But Aquila, it's me... Vittorio Veneto.
I don't believe you. Marco Polo already tried this trick once! It didn't work then, and it definitely won't work now!
Nothing you say will make me trust you! Ladies, prepare for battle!
Wait, Aquila! I know you must be confused since I awoke you so suddenly. Um...
Please don't be rash. Just take a deep breath with me... It's really me, Veneto. I've borrowed this conceptual weapon to break the illusion and get you out.
Y'know, that DID sound a lot like what Veneto would say...
You think so?
What is this "illusion" you speak of? I don't understand what you mean.
Have you not been having unusual dreams? Maybe you've run into strange people, or noticed odd things happening.
Uh, no? The weirdest thing that's happened lately is you...
So... nothing strange has happened there whatsoever?
What about from abroad? Have you received any calls or unusual messages?
No. All our communications with Rome and nearby fleets have been perfectly normal.
(Did the illusions have absolutely zero impact on our country?! There's no way that can be true!)
(Oh, I get it! The perpetrator was so afraid of Sardegna's glory that they dared not touch us!)
(Yes, that explains it!)

Akagi's Sister.say: In a Tribunal hideout, shipgirls who had awoken from the dream were having a meeting.
Elizabeth and Veneto are steadily dispelling illusions across Europa. All that remains is the Northern Parliament.
Ah, them... I've heard that Soyuz transferred the chain of command over to the Pacific.
This move seems calculated. I think we should refrain from employing our conceptual weapon on their shipgirls.
Next topic, then. I've received intel that some circumstances behind the illusions have come to light.
There is no dancing around it – fragments of the Watatsumi played a role in this incident.
It must be stated that the Sakura Empire had no plans for any incident such as this...
Nobody is suspecting your country. It's Akagi who's suspected.
Akagi?
Let me fill you in. Many people have reported seeing someone who goes by the name of "Amagi" in their dreams.
Amagi. The lead ship of her class and sister to Akagi.
Correct. Friedrich and I looked into her once. For what's relevant to this conversation, Amagi died.
